articles.title: The Subscription Economy: How Recurring Revenue Is Reshaping Business Forever
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: From Netflix to SaaS empires — how the shift from ownership to access is rewriting the rules of how companies make money.
articles.content: Something profound has happened to the way businesses make money. The transaction is dying. In its place, a new economic architecture is rising: one built not on one-time sales, but on ongoing relationships and perpetual access.

articles.title: Gut Health: Why Your Second Brain May Be Running Your Life
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Inside the gut-brain axis — how 100 trillion microbes quietly shape your mood, immunity, and mental health.
articles.content: The gut contains more neurons than your spinal cord, produces 95% of the body's serotonin, and talks directly to your brain. Neuroscience is only now catching up.

articles.title: The Science of Sleep: What Happens Inside Your Brain While You Rest
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Every night your brain runs a maintenance cycle no waking activity can replicate — and the neuroscience of why is extraordinary.
articles.content: While you sleep, your brain is running at full tilt — consolidating memories, flushing neurotoxic waste, and cycling through states as different from each other as waking life is from dreaming.

articles.title: The Psychology of Color in Design: How Hues Shape Human Behavior
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Color is design's most silent persuader — triggering emotion and shaping decisions before a single word is consciously read.
articles.content: Before you read a single word on a website, your brain has already made a decision about how you feel — shaped entirely by color.

articles.title: The Rise of Edge AI: Why the Future of Intelligence Is Offline
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: AI no longer needs the cloud to be powerful. On-device intelligence is rewriting the rules of privacy, speed, and connectivity forever.
articles.content: Your smartphone is now smarter than the supercomputers of fifteen years ago — and it no longer needs to phone home to prove it.

articles.title: Why the Creator Economy Is Rewriting the Rules of Work
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: More than 50 million people now call themselves creators. What happens when passion becomes profession at scale?
articles.content: A generation ago, you needed a studio, a label, a publisher, or a network to reach an audience.

articles.title: Quantum Computing Explained: The Race to Rewrite the Rules of Physics
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Inside the labs where scientists are harnessing quantum mechanics to build computers of the future.
articles.content: Inside the most advanced physics labs on earth, a new type of computer is being born.

articles.title: The Art of Storytelling in Modern Society: Why Narrative Still Rules Everything
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: From ancient myths to digital narratives — how storytelling remains humanity's most powerful tool for connection, meaning-making, and cultural transmission.
articles.content: Before written language — before alphabets — there was story. We are creatures who make sense of the world through narrative.

articles.title: The Web3 Illusion: Separating Hype from Genuine Innovation
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Blockchain promised revolution. Reality is far messier. A critical examination of Web3 hype, fundamental trade-offs, and where decentralization actually matters.
articles.content: The promise of Web3 was revolutionary: a decentralized internet owned by its users, free from corporate gatekeepers.

articles.title: How Personal Branding Is Becoming the New Resume
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: In an era of radical visibility, how you show up online has become more powerful than any credential you can print on a traditional resume.
articles.content: A short summary of the article...

articles.title: Understanding Emotional Intelligence: The Science Behind Feeling
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: EQ isn't a buzzword — it's a measurable set of skills that shapes your relationships, career, and wellbeing. Here's what the science actually says.
articles.content: There is a certain type of person who fails every standardised test, struggles with formal education, and yet navigates the world with an almost uncanny effectiveness. Today, psychology finally has good language for this.

articles.title: The Philosophy of Minimalism: Why Less Has Always Been a Radical Idea
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Minimalism is not an aesthetic trend. It is a centuries-old philosophical tradition — and a serious response to the question of what makes a life worth living.
articles.content: Strip the Muji catalogues and capsule wardrobes away, and you find something considerably more demanding: a centuries-old insistence that most of what we accumulate does not add to life — it costs life.

articles.title: The Mythology of Time: How Different Cultures Experience the Clock
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Across cultures and centuries, humanity has shaped wildly different relationships with time — and those differences remake how we think, plan, and live our lives.
articles.content: There is a moment — familiar to anyone who has landed in a radically different culture — where the clock stops making sense. Not because it is broken, but because the entire social grammar around time has changed.

articles.title: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: How Machines Are Learning to Think
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: From deep learning breakthroughs to the ethical questions keeping researchers up at night — a deep dive into the technology reshaping civilization as we know it.
articles.content: Artificial intelligence is no longer science fiction. It is reshaping industries, economies, and the very nature of what it means to create.

articles.title: The Principles of Timeless Design That Never Go Out of Style
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Why Dieter Rams' ten commandments of good design remain as relevant today as they were in 1978.
articles.content: Good design is honest. It does not make a product appear more innovative, powerful or valuable than it really is.

articles.title: How Cities Shape the Human Mind and the Way We Think
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: The surprising neuroscience behind urban environments and why dense cities make us smarter and more creative.
articles.content: Something happens to the human mind in a city. The density of people, ideas, and accidents creates a cognitive environment unlike anything our ancestors knew.

articles.title: Typography in the Digital Age: Why Letters Still Matter More Than Ever
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: How the craft of type-setting has evolved from Gutenberg's press to responsive variable fonts on modern screens.
articles.content: Before images, before color, before motion — there was text. And the way that text looks shapes how we feel about every word we read.

articles.title: The Climate Crisis and the Innovators Racing to Fix It
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: From direct air capture to fusion reactors — the technologies that could redefine humanity's relationship with the planet.
articles.content: The climate emergency is no longer a future problem. It is today's emergency, demanding today's solutions.

articles.title: The Quiet Revolution in Mental Health Technology
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Smartphone apps and AI therapists are changing access to mental healthcare — but at what cost to human connection?
articles.content: One in four people will experience a mental health condition in their lifetime. For most of history, the path to treatment was narrow and expensive.
